---
title: "Test emocionales Ratones"
author: "Juliana Zapata"
date: "11/21/2023"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      fig.align = "center")
# Manejo de datos, gráficos, colores
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(jcolors)
library(DT)
library(plotly)
library(ggpubr)
library(broom)
library(tidyr)
library(corrplot)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Análisis multivariado
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# Lectura de datos General C57
```{r}

datos <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Estadística Naty/Datos pruebas emocionales filtrada.xlsx", 
     sheet = "CAMPO ABIERTO C57")
datos
```

## Descriptivo

```{r}
library(DT)
datos %>% 
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Sexo)) %>% 
  gather(key = "Variable", value = "value", -Grupo) %>% 
  group_by(Variable, Grupo) %>%
  summarise(Promedio = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3),
            DE = round(sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3),
            Mínimo = round(min(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3),
            Máximo = round(max(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(CV = round((DE/Promedio)*100, digits = 3)) %>% 
  datatable(extensions = 'Buttons', options = list(
     dom = 'Bfrtip',
     buttons = c('csv', 'excel', 'pdf')))
```

## Distribuciones originales

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
datos %>% 
  select_if(is.numeric) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor") %>% 
  ggplot(data = ., aes(x = valor)) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  geom_density()
```

## Shapiro-Wilk 


```{r}
datos %>% 
  select_if(is.numeric) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor") %>% 
  group_by(variable) %>% 
  summarise(valor = list(valor)) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(variable) %>% 
  mutate(shapiro_valorP = round(shapiro.test(unlist(valor))$p.value, digits = 5),
         Resultado = if_else(shapiro_valorP <= 0.05, true = "No normalidad",
                             false = "Sí normalidad"))
``` 
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=15}
datos%>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL)) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor", -Grupo, -Sexo) %>% 
  group_by(variable, Grupo, Sexo) %>% 
  summarise(promedio = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),
            de = sd(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Grupo, y = promedio, color = Sexo,
             ymin  = promedio - de,
             ymax = promedio + de)) +
facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free", ncol = 3) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_jcolors(palette = "pal7") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "snow4")
   )
```
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
datos %>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Grupo)) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor", -Sexo) %>% 
  group_by(variable, Sexo) %>% 
  summarise(promedio = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),
            de = sd(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Sexo, y = promedio, color = Sexo,
             ymin  = promedio - de,
             ymax = promedio + de)) +
facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free", ncol = 4) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_jcolors(palette = "pal7") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "snow4")
   )
```

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
datos %>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Sexo)) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor", -Grupo) %>% 
  group_by(variable, Grupo) %>% 
  summarise(promedio = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),
            de = sd(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Grupo, y = promedio, color = Grupo,
             ymin  = promedio - de,
             ymax = promedio + de)) +
facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free", ncol = 4) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_jcolors(palette = "pal7") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "snow4")
   )
```

##Correlaciones

### Datos globales

```{r}
datoscor <- datos %>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Grupo, Sexo))
datoscor
```
### Matriz
```{r}
matriz_cor <- cor(datoscor)
as.data.frame(matriz_cor)
```


### Gráfico de correlaciones
```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
library(Hmisc)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(corrplot)
mtx_cor <- datoscor %>% 
  cor(method = "spearman") 

corrplot((type="upper"),
  corr = mtx_cor,
  method = "circle",
  col = brewer.pal(n = 3, name = "Spectral"),
  tl.col = "black",
  tl.srt = 50,
  tl.cex = 0.9
  )
```

## Base de datos para Comparaciones de medias

```{r}
datos
datos$Grupo <- factor (datos$Grupo )
datos$Sexo <- factor (datos$Sexo )
```

## Anova Grupo vs Cepa DTR

```{r}
datos$Grupo <- factor ( datos$Grupo )
modelo1 <- aov((DTR) ~ Grupo*Sexo, data = datos)
summary( modelo1 ) 
```

### Resultados Tukey


```{r}
TukeyHSD(modelo1)
```
### Gráfico de Tukey
```{r, fig.width=13}
par(mar=c(5, 15, 2, 1))
plot(TukeyHSD(modelo1), las = 1)
```

## T.test Grupo

```{r}
datos$Grupo <- factor ( datos$Grupo )
 t.test((DTR) ~ Grupo, data = datos)

```
## T.test Sexo

```{r}
datos$Sexo <- factor ( datos$Sexo )
 t.test((DTR) ~ Sexo, data = datos)

```

## Krukal Wallis

```{r}
mod1 <- kruskal.test(DTR ~ Grupo, data = datos)
mod1
```

- **Prueba U de Mann-Whitney:**

```{r}
wilcox.test(TZE ~ Grupo, data = datos)
```
# Comparación de medias Grupo
```{r}
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
datos %>%
  wilcox_test(TZE ~ Grupo, paired = FALSE) %>%
  print()
```

# Comparación de medias Grupo
```{r}
ggplot(datos, aes(x = Grupo, y = TZE))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  stat_compare_means()
```

# Lectura de datos General BalbC
```{r}

datos1 <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Estadística Naty/Datos pruebas emocionales filtrada.xlsx", 
     sheet = "CAMPO ABIERTO BALB-C")
datos1
```

## Descriptivo

```{r}
library(DT)
datos1 %>% 
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Sexo)) %>% 
  gather(key = "Variable", value = "value", -Grupo) %>% 
  group_by(Variable, Grupo) %>%
  summarise(Promedio = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3),
            DE = round(sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3),
            Mínimo = round(min(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3),
            Máximo = round(max(value, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(CV = round((DE/Promedio)*100, digits = 3)) %>% 
  datatable(extensions = 'Buttons', options = list(
     dom = 'Bfrtip',
     buttons = c('csv', 'excel', 'pdf')))
```

## Distribuciones originales

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
datos1 %>% 
  select_if(is.numeric) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor") %>% 
  ggplot(data = ., aes(x = valor)) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  geom_density()
```

## Shapiro-Wilk 


```{r}
datos1 %>% 
  select_if(is.numeric) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor") %>% 
  group_by(variable) %>% 
  summarise(valor = list(valor)) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(variable) %>% 
  mutate(shapiro_valorP = round(shapiro.test(unlist(valor))$p.value, digits = 5),
         Resultado = if_else(shapiro_valorP <= 0.05, true = "No normalidad",
                             false = "Sí normalidad"))
``` 
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=15}
datos1%>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL)) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor", -Grupo, -Sexo) %>% 
  group_by(variable, Grupo, Sexo) %>% 
  summarise(promedio = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),
            de = sd(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Grupo, y = promedio, color = Sexo,
             ymin  = promedio - de,
             ymax = promedio + de)) +
facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free", ncol = 3) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_jcolors(palette = "pal7") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "snow4")
   )
```
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
datos1 %>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Grupo)) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor", -Sexo) %>% 
  group_by(variable, Sexo) %>% 
  summarise(promedio = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),
            de = sd(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Sexo, y = promedio, color = Sexo,
             ymin  = promedio - de,
             ymax = promedio + de)) +
facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free", ncol = 4) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_jcolors(palette = "pal7") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "snow4")
   )
```

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=8}
datos1 %>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Sexo)) %>% 
  gather(key = "variable", value = "valor", -Grupo) %>% 
  group_by(variable, Grupo) %>% 
  summarise(promedio = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),
            de = sd(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Grupo, y = promedio, color = Grupo,
             ymin  = promedio - de,
             ymax = promedio + de)) +
facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free", ncol = 4) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_jcolors(palette = "pal7") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "snow4")
   )
```

##Correlaciones

### Datos globales

```{r}
datoscor <- datos1 %>%
  select(-c(ANIMAL, Grupo, Sexo))
datoscor
```
### Matriz
```{r}
matriz_cor <- cor(datoscor)
as.data.frame(matriz_cor)
```


### Gráfico de correlaciones
```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
library(Hmisc)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(corrplot)
mtx_cor <- datoscor %>% 
  cor(method = "spearman") 

corrplot((type="upper"),
  corr = mtx_cor,
  method = "circle",
  col = brewer.pal(n = 3, name = "Spectral"),
  tl.col = "black",
  tl.srt = 50,
  tl.cex = 0.9
  )
```

## Base de datos para Comparaciones de medias

```{r}
datos1
datos1$Grupo <- factor (datos1$Grupo )
datos1$Sexo <- factor (datos1$Sexo )
```

## Anova Grupo vs Cepa DTR

```{r}
datos1$Grupo <- factor ( datos1$Grupo )
modelo1 <- aov((DTR) ~ Grupo*Sexo, data = datos1)
summary( modelo1 ) 
```

### Resultados Tukey


```{r}
TukeyHSD(modelo1)
```
### Gráfico de Tukey
```{r, fig.width=13}
par(mar=c(5, 15, 2, 1))
plot(TukeyHSD(modelo1), las = 1)
```

## T.test Grupo

```{r}
datos1$Grupo <- factor ( datos1$Grupo )
 t.test((DTR) ~ Grupo, data = datos1)

```
## T.test CEPA

```{r}
datos1$Sexo <- factor ( datos1$Sexo )
 t.test((DTR) ~ Sexo, data = datos1)
```

## Krukal Wallis

```{r}
mod1 <- kruskal.test(DTR ~ Grupo, data = datos1)
mod1
```

- **Prueba U de Mann-Whitney:**

```{r}
wilcox.test(TZC ~ Grupo, data = datos1)
```
# Comparación de medias Grupo
```{r}
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
datos1 %>%
  wilcox_test(VLP ~ Grupo, paired = FALSE) %>%
  print()
```

# Comparación de medias Grupo
```{r}
ggplot(datos1, aes(x = Grupo, y = TZP))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  stat_compare_means()
```
